videos.description:
Today I put Copilot Cowork to the test in a real scenario I run into all the time.

I had a slide deck for a conference presentation that needed to match the official event template. Instead of manually reformatting every slide, I wanted to see if Copilot Cowork could take my existing slides and automatically align them to the template deck.

So, I tried it live.

In this episode of Can it Cowork?, I walk through the experiment step-by-step and see if Copilot Cowork can actually handle this real

videos.title: Most Microsoft Fabric Workspaces Are Organized Wrong
videos.description:
Most teams treat **Microsoft Fabric workspaces like folders**.

A place to organize reports.
A place to store dashboards.
A place to group projects.

But workspaces aren’t folders.

They define **accountability boundaries**.

In this video, Patrick walks through how he evaluates **workspace structure in Microsoft Fabric** and why poor workspace design leads to:

• unclear ownership
• project sprawl
• permission chaos
• blurred Dev vs Test vs Prod boundaries

videos.title: The AI Subsidy Era is Over
videos.description:
End of the AI subsidy era forces a shift from flat-fee subscriptions to usage-based pricing as agentic, token-heavy workflows escalate inference costs.  
Anthropic, OpenAI, Microsoft, and GitHub Copilot reveal capacity constraints, model metering, and steep price multipliers with direct impacts on vendor competition and market expectations.
